title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends For Diy Car Maintenance Signals Rising Auto Sector Interest
thesis: An increase in searches for DIY car maintenance often precedes higher demand for auto parts and services, reflecting consumer stress on vehicle upkeep costs. Auto parts and industrial sectors benefit from increased vehicle maintenance activity.
signal_logic: Enter long XLI when 3-week moving average of 'DIY car maintenance' Google Trends rises more than 15% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLI when 3-week moving average of 'DIY car maintenance' Google Trends rises more than 15% week-over-week Exit: Exit after 4 weeks or if the 2-week moving average declines below 5% week-over-week
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly Google Trends data often shows repeated interest spikes in consumer maintenance topics.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends for 'DIY car maintenance' signals rising auto sector interest
schema.behavior: An increase in searches for DIY car maintenance often precedes higher demand for auto parts and services, reflecting consumer stress on vehicle upkeep costs.

market_impact.rationale: Auto parts and industrial sectors benefit from increased vehicle maintenance activity.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long XLI when 3-week moving average of 'DIY car maintenance' Google Trends rises more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 4 weeks or if the 2-week moving average declines below 5% week-over-week

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly Google Trends data often shows repeated interest spikes in consumer maintenance topics.

title: Daily Jump In Google Trends For Emergency Plumber Near Me Signals Consumer Home Repair Stress
thesis: Sudden increases in emergency plumbing searches indicate acute home repair demand which often benefits consumer discretionary sectors. Consumer discretionary companies supplying home services and repairs gain from heightened demand.
signal_logic: Enter long XLY when daily search volume for 'emergency plumber near me' exceeds 2 std deviations above 30-day mean
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLY when daily search volume for 'emergency plumber near me' exceeds 2 std deviations above 30-day mean Exit: Exit after 5 trading days or once volume falls below 1 std deviation above mean
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends daily search interest via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily home emergency repair searches tend to spike frequently due to accidents and weather.

schema.idea: Daily jump in Google Trends for 'emergency plumber near me' signals consumer home repair stress
schema.behavior: Sudden increases in emergency plumbing searches indicate acute home repair demand which often benefits consumer discretionary sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary companies supplying home services and repairs gain from heightened demand.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long XLY when daily search volume for 'emergency plumber near me' exceeds 2 std deviations above 30-day mean
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or once volume falls below 1 std deviation above mean

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily home emergency repair searches tend to spike frequently due to accidents and weather.

title: Daily Spike In Freight Logistics Rss Counts Mentioning Port Congestion Signals Supply Chain Stress
thesis: A surge in news mentioning port congestion indicates worsening logistics bottlenecks that can pressure industrial supply chains. Industrial and transportation sectors face input delays and higher costs during port congestion episodes.
data_sources: RSS news feed counts for freight and shipping
signal_logic: Enter short XLI when daily RSS counts for 'port congestion' exceed 3 standard deviations above 30-day mean
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLI when daily RSS counts for 'port congestion' exceed 3 standard deviations above 30-day mean Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if counts revert below 1.5 std deviations
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS news feed counts for freight and shipping via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: News cycles frequently report on port delays and congestion spikes.

schema.idea: Daily spike in freight logistics RSS counts mentioning 'port congestion' signals supply chain stress

data.source: RSS news feed counts for freight and shipping

schema.behavior: A surge in news mentioning port congestion indicates worsening logistics bottlenecks that can pressure industrial supply chains.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial and transportation sectors face input delays and higher costs during port congestion episodes.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short XLI when daily RSS counts for 'port congestion' exceed 3 standard deviations above 30-day mean
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if counts revert below 1.5 std deviations

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: News cycles frequently report on port delays and congestion spikes.

entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLI when 2-week Google Trends for 'warehouse worker strike' rises by more than 20% week-over-week Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when trend drops below 10% weekly increase

risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor unrest is a recurrent theme with measurable weekly search interest surges.

schema.behavior: Increasing search interest in warehouse worker strikes often precedes labor disruptions impacting industrial and consumer goods sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Labor strikes can reduce output and increase costs in industrial supply chains.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short XLI when 2-week Google Trends for 'warehouse worker strike' rises by more than 20% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when trend drops below 10% weekly increase

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Travel disruptions often cause sharp daily spikes in cancellation-related searches.

title: Weekly Rise In Fred Industrial Production Input Prices Signals Rising Input Cost Pressure
thesis: Increasing input prices for industry drive margin pressure and cost-push inflation concerns. Rising input costs compress industrial sector profitability.
data_sources: FRED API series for industrial input prices
signal_logic: Enter short XLI when weekly input price index rises by more than 1% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short XLI when weekly input price index rises by more than 1% week-over-week Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when weekly growth drops below 0.3%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED API series for industrial input prices via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Input price indices update regularly with measurable week-over-week changes.

schema.idea: Weekly rise in FRED industrial production input prices signals rising input cost pressure

data.source: FRED API series for industrial input prices

schema.behavior: Increasing input prices for industry drive margin pressure and cost-push inflation concerns.

market_impact.rationale: Rising input costs compress industrial sector profitability.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short XLI when weekly input price index rises by more than 1% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when weekly growth drops below 0.3%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Input price indices update regularly with measurable week-over-week changes.
